target: D:\Project\video2pdf\newskill-kimi\全程_Tim_刘润_进化者访谈_跟着tim一起拆解_爆款内容是怎么_炼_成的_20260702_232511\section_05.tex

findings.issue: The title promises a three-party commercialization pyramid, and the strongest body material does center on advertiser, creator, and audience value. Later subsections on failure judgment, platform risk, and head effects broaden the chapter into a wider operating-system analysis.
findings.recommendation: Add one bridging sentence before the platform-risk subsection explaining how platform and budget shocks pressure the three-party value triangle, so the broader risk material remains visibly tied to the title claim.

findings.location: Subsection '三块收入：广告、商业服务/直播、电商'
findings.issue: The three revenue categories are distinct enough for teaching, but '商业服务/直播' combines two mechanisms with different economics: project-based service delivery and live-commerce or live-event operations.
findings.recommendation: Clarify whether livestreaming is treated as a service-delivery format or as a separate monetization channel, or briefly explain why the source groups them together.

findings.issue: The ASR caveat is useful for source discipline, but its placement immediately after the opening figure briefly interrupts the teaching progression from claim to mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Consider moving the caveat into a footnote or a short source note at the end of the section unless integration rules require it near the top.

audit.evaluation_context: Teaching-PDF section checkpoint: validate that this Bilibili chapter draft has a clear teaching claim, coherent subsection hierarchy, distinct same-level groups, source-grounded examples, and a reader-facing takeaway before integration.
